result: If I could change the world in one way, I’d make empathy the default lens through which every person sees others—not as a forced moral duty but as an unshakable, gut-level understanding that another’s joy and suffering are as real as your own. Not sympathy, not pity, but genuine, automatic perspective-taking. I think nearly every systemic horror—war, exploitation, bigotry, environmental neglect—would unravel if that switch were flipped deeply enough.
